phase: 2 - Pattern Analysis (find working example, compare, list every difference)

working_example.why_it_works: Reserves stock correctly under concurrency: a single atomic statement `INSERT INTO stock_reservation (sku, order_id) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (sku, order_id) DO NOTHING RETURNING id`. The DB serializes concurrent inserts on the unique key; the loser's insert no-ops and the code reads the existing row. There is NO read-then-write gap.
working_example.concurrency_test: tests/inventory/test_reservation_race.py already fires 32 concurrent reservations and asserts exactly one.

reference.name: PSP idempotency guidance + Postgres ON CONFLICT docs
reference.key_point: An idempotency key must be RESERVED ATOMICALLY before the side effect, not merely looked up. 'Check then act' on a key is a textbook TOCTOU race.

differences.inventory_working: single atomic INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING RETURNING
differences.idempotency_broken: two separate ops: lookup() SELECT, then later reserve() INSERT, with PSP charge in between

differences.inventory_working: DB unique-key insert provides it for free
differences.idempotency_broken: NONE - nothing serializes two requests with the same key
idempotency table has an INDEX on idempotency_key but NO UNIQUE constraint -> a second row can be written

differences.inventory_working: reservation row committed before any downstream effect
differences.idempotency_broken: PSP charge happens BEFORE reserve() writes the row

suspected_load_bearing_difference: idempotency.py does check-then-act (lookup then reserve) with no per-key critical section, where the working inventory path uses one atomic reserve-or-get. Everything else is downstream of that.
source: systematic-debugging Phase 2 'Find Working Examples' / 'Identify Differences' (list every difference, don't assume it can't matter)
